title: Essay on NGE
wikitext:
[]: Essay on NGE             Quote Post by Vulkurt » Sun Jan 09, 2005 8:05 am [url]http://www.efn.org/~dredmond/[/url] Look under essays. Second one. Haven't read it yet, but it may be interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Jan 09, 2005 8:32 am Moderately interesting. It gives a lot of historical background information which is interesting, but I felt like it could have gone deeper into Evangelion lore. Also is this true Nadia is the model for Misato Katsuragi

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Sun Jan 09, 2005 10:55 am It was my understanding that she was the model for Shinji. I'm not joking. There eye's are drawn the same way (Sadamoto say's so).

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 09, 2005 12:19 pm Ah yes, Dennis Redmond. I'm currently reading the sections on Evangelion in his book "The World is Watching" . He's a smart fellow, much of his own ideas of Eva are unique to himself. Within what I've read of his book, the only thing which I found to be blatantly misinterpreting Eva is when he describes the Angels as a "mysterious alien species from another dimension". Aside from that, what he's written on Eva hasn't really seemed very earth shattering or interesting. I've been more intrigued by fan essays by the likes of Brian Shea, Amanda Wells, and MDWigs. Mr. Redmond's comments concerning Misato's character design are indeed false. As Sadamoto stated in "My Thoughts at the Moment" , Nadia is the model for Shinji, not Misato. Spreaking in concreate terms, his eyes are a girl's eyes. I drew them exactly as I drew Nadia's eyes. He's a male Nadia, just as if I had given Nadia a masculine makeover. Lengthen the eyelashes and change the hair style, and you have Nadia.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Sun Jan 09, 2005 12:28 pm Yeah I read it a while ago and it's pretty good. I also mentioned it a bit ago (probably should of gave it it's own topics). [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=491[/url] Yeah it was really interesting and considering it's free, y'all should read it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 09, 2005 2:37 pm

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 09, 2005 3:00 pm Glorious.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Sun Jan 09, 2005 3:39 pm I really wanna know whats going on in the botton left o.O

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Sun Jan 09, 2005 3:39 pm Yep, it's kinda creepy now. Maybe he has hormone problems.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Sun Jan 09, 2005 3:40 pm Shinji IS A DICKGIRL

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jan 09, 2005 4:22 pm i think it looks more like Misato..if you compare a screenshot where Misato is with Kaji and Ritsuko in an old photo is exactly the same..

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 09, 2005 4:52 pm max3000 wrote: Shinji IS A DICKGIRL

[]:             Quote Post by chocomanji » Sun Jan 09, 2005 6:46 pm That author's pretty interesting (I found the book for EM in our college's library), he pretty much proves that you can make a living/hobby by just watching a crapload of films and television....if anything, his obscure references to other films and shows in his analysis of evangelion made me think...and laugh.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 09, 2005 7:28 pm max3000 wrote: Shinji IS A DICKGIRL

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 09, 2005 8:03 pm God damnit, I was trying to forget about that.

[]:             Quote Post by Tabris » Sun Jan 09, 2005 9:13 pm Does anyone have any idea what that image from EoE is meant to represent?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 09, 2005 10:36 pm Make a thread for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Jan 10, 2005 2:14 am Again?

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Jan 10, 2005 3:02 am Hexon.Arq wrote: Again?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Jan 10, 2005 3:17 pm After Shinji sees the spermy legion of Rei swim past him, there is a quick back-and-forth pan across a cluster of entities; to the right it is orange, to the left it is inverted.
